description: Evaluates identity management service maturity—identity stores, subject and object profiles, human and machine identity lifecycle, third-party vendor identity, break glass, KPIs/KRIs, and SOC logging of identity lifecycle changes.

capabilities.name: Service exists and is in good condition
capabilities.description: An identity management service exists and is reviewed on a regular cadence for efficacy.

capabilities.name: KPIs/KRIs
capabilities.description: Identity management KPIs and KRIs—including provisioning velocity, orphaned identity rate, and machine identity coverage—are documented and reviewed at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: Mean time to deprovision after role change or termination
Machine and workload identity inventory metrics

capabilities.name: Identity store
capabilities.description: A centralized identity store manages enterprise, third-party, and machine identities with automation, cloud IdP integration, SCIM, configuration review, and enforcement point integration.
evaluation_focus: Enforcement point integration with identity store
Cloud identity provider and directory federation

capabilities.name: Identity store custodians
capabilities.description: Separate roles manage the identity store and the identities themselves.
evaluation_focus: Separate roles for identity store and identity management

capabilities.name: Subject and object profiles
capabilities.description: Subject and object profiles are created via formalized processes considering environment, context, and scope; automated where appropriate; reviewed regularly; and used in policy creation at enforcement points.
evaluation_focus: Automated subject and object profile creation
Subject and object profile creation review
Enforcement points use profiles in policy

capabilities.name: Identity provisioning and deprovisioning
capabilities.description: Identity provisioning and deprovisioning based on profiles and triggers are formalized, automated, and reviewed at least quarterly.

capabilities.name: Third-party vendor identity lifecycle
capabilities.description: Third-party identity provisioning and deprovisioning are documented, automated from profile and triggers, and reviewed at least quarterly.

capabilities.name: Machine and workload identity lifecycle
capabilities.description: Service principals, workloads, automation accounts, and non-human identities are inventoried, provisioned, and deprovisioned with lifecycle governance—not ad hoc shared accounts.
evaluation_focus: Formalized machine and workload identity provisioning
Automated machine identity deprovisioning on workload retirement
Machine identity inventory and ownership attribution
Governance of automation and AI agent identities as first-class subjects

capabilities.name: Emergency break glass
capabilities.description: An emergency break glass process exists when the identity store is unreachable and is reviewed at least quarterly.

capabilities.name: Identity lifecycle logging
capabilities.description: Identity management and lifecycle changes are logged securely to the SOC, including dynamic profile changes and anomalous identity modifications.
evaluation_focus: Documented identity lifecycle logging to SOC
Secure identity lifecycle logging to SOC
Dynamic identity profile changes to SOC

evaluation.resiliency_weight_policy: Ask stakeholder for per-capability weights when adding a new evaluation service. This service uses weight 1 for all capabilities.
evaluation.pillar_policy: Some services may have no Technology capabilities; in those cases, do not compute or report a Technology pillar score.
